ip_outcome_note: suspected_ip was empty string — no IP verification task.

trivia_bullets.text: Eerie mechanic was internally called 'Afraid' during Vision Design before being renamed. Set Design removed its earlier caring about Horrors and Nightmares, narrowing it to enchantments and Rooms unlocking.
sources: Wizards of the Coast: 'Top of the Duskmourning, Part 1'

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text delivers black comedy: Duskmourn's crabs have adapted to burrow 'into everything from carpet to flesh' since the House has no beaches. One of the few comedic flavor texts in the DSK set.

Costs {U}, 0/3. One of DSK's most mana-efficient Eerie payoffs: mills 2 on every enchantment entry or Room unlock. A single Room can trigger it twice (enter + fully unlock) = 4 mills. EDHREC rank 11,849, best Commander adoption among these five cards. Also legal in The Lord of the Rings Commander format (tlr).
sources: Wizards of the Coast: 'Duskmourn: House of Horror Mechanics'

trivia_bullets.text: Art by John Tedrick. The skull-carapace reads as biological horror: the crab's body IS a skull rather than merely skull-patterned, merging the haunted-house and body-horror registers that define DSK's two core horror modes.

related_cards.name: Restless Ridgeline
related_cards.note: Example Room card; each full unlock triggers Skullcrab twice

related_cards.name: Valgavoth, Terror Eater
related_cards.note: Animating demon of the House; Skullcrab flavor gestures at his domain

related_cards.name: Ghostly Keybearer
related_cards.note: Another blue uncommon that synergizes with Room mechanics
